claims.statement: MESA-import benchmark support requires profile hashes and model metadata such as inlist and network references.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: MESA reference for profile-backed stellar models.

claims.statement: Benchmark support should compare integrated nuclear luminosity and surface luminosity with an explicit closure tolerance.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: Stellar structure context for comparing profile and observable luminosity.

claims.statement: Stage 2 candidate benchmark support requires uncertainty propagation or an explicit blocker.

sources.citation: Carcassi, G., Landini, G. and Aidala, C. (2024). Classical mechanics as the high-entropy limit of quantum mechanics.
sources.note: Motivates uncertainty-aware ensemble framing for QST-adjacent claims.

claims.statement: Benchmark dominant fusion channel should be derived from integrated component epsilon contributions.

sources.citation: Adelberger, E. G. et al. (2011). Solar fusion cross sections II: the pp chain and CNO cycles.
sources.note: Reference for pp-chain and CNO-cycle microphysics context.

claims.statement: Benchmark fusion-zone radii should be derived from cumulative nuclear luminosity.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.equation: r90 encloses 90 percent of cumulative nuclear luminosity
sources.note: Profile energy-generation context for fusion-zone diagnostics.

claims.statement: Effective surface temperature is an observable closure check and should not be treated as core or fusion-zone temperature.
hSpectralFit in benchmark reports is calibration-only and cannot claim a new or varying Planck constant.

sources.equation: h = 6.62607015e-34 J s exactly in the revised SI
sources.note: Reference for exact Planck constant value.

claims.statement: Neutron-star benchmark fixtures are compact-object dense-matter context and not stellar fusion benchmarks.

sources.citation: Khomenko, V. et al. (2020). Turbulent, pinned superfluids in neutron stars and pulsar glitch recoveries.

claims.statement: Gaia-like star maps are population-prior context for benchmarks and not direct quantum-spacetime evidence.

sources.citation: Gaia Collaboration (2023). Gaia Data Release 3: Summary of the content and survey properties.

claims.statement: StarSim fusion benchmarks can support astrophysical priors but cannot directly support ER=EPR or promote QST/warp claims to CL0-CL4.

sources.citation: Maldacena, J. and Susskind, L. (2013). Cool horizons for entangled black holes.
sources.equation: ER=EPR context only; no stellar benchmark equation is asserted.
sources.note: Boundary source for separating ER=EPR from StarSim benchmarks.
